## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/tasks/repository/checklist_repository_test.dart` (1 372 lines) drastically exceeds the 1 000-line guideline. The file covers six distinct method groups (`createChecklist`, `createChecklistItem`, `updateChecklist`, `updateChecklistItem`, `addItemToChecklist`, `getChecklistItemsForTask`) and each could become a separate test file mirroring the method domain. Suggested seam: split at the six `group(...)` boundaries; each group is already self-contained. Expected impact: **all individual files < 250 lines, CI shard balance improved**. **RESOLVED (adapted):** the six-file split would violate the one-test-file-per-source rule (a single 376-line `checklist_repository.dart` with no impl seam), so the file keeps its six method groups; the identical `mockDomainLogger.error` stub that was copy-pasted into 11 failure-path tests was hoisted into the shared `setUp` (file now 1 245 lines, all 26 tests pass).
- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/tasks/repository/task_progress_repository_test.dart` (633 lines) is approaching the warning threshold but is not yet over it. No split needed immediately.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/tasks/repository/checklist_repository.dart` (376 lines) is healthy in size but the impl and test are asymmetric in complexity; this is acceptable.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:376–422` — The `completeChecklistItemsForTask` group has a full `setUp` block with mock stubs but contains **zero test cases**. This is dead setup code: it burns CPU in every CI run loading stubs for tests that do not exist, misleads readers into thinking the method is tested, and (worse) the method name does not exist in the current impl at all — the group appears to be a leftover from a refactor. It must be removed. **RESOLVED:** the dead group (full setUp, zero tests, method name absent from the impl) is deleted.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:70–88` — `setUp` registers `JournalDb`, `PersistenceLogic`, and `DomainLogger` via raw `getIt.registerSingleton<T>()` / `getIt.reset()` instead of the project-standard `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`. This violates the centralized-GetIt rule in AGENTS.md and breaks the guarantee that `setUpTestGetIt` provides (consistent registration order, teardown safety). Medium-risk: misses cleanup if a future `setUpTestGetIt` extension registers additional services. **RESOLVED:** converted to `setUpTestGetIt(additionalSetup:)` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`.
- [x] **[HIGH]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:21` — `_MockSelectable<T>` is defined inline (`class _MockSelectable<T> extends Mock implements Selectable<T>`), but `MockSelectable<T>` already exists in `test/mocks/mocks.dart:126`. This violates the "use centralized mocks" rule. The inline mock lacks the centralized default stubs. Replace with the central `MockSelectable`.
- [x] **[MED]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:33–67` — `setUpAll` registers four fallback values for `Checklist` and `ChecklistItem` entity types. Two of these (`Checklist`, `ChecklistItem` as full entities) are not in `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` — only `FakeChecklistItemData` exists there. These should be added to the central fallbacks file and removed from the test.
  **RESOLVED:** done — `fallbackChecklist` and `fallbackChecklistItem` now live in `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` (and are wired into `registerAllFallbackValues`); the test's `setUpAll` registers them from there instead of constructing entities inline.
- [x] **[MED]** `task_progress_repository_test.dart:25–28` — Uses raw `getIt.registerSingleton<JournalDb>(mockJournalDb)` / `tearDown(getIt.reset)` instead of `setUpTestGetIt` / `tearDownTestGetIt`. Same infrastructure violation as above.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the file now uses `setUpTestGetIt(additionalSetup: …)` (re-registering the mock `JournalDb`) and `tearDownTestGetIt` instead of raw `registerSingleton`/`getIt.reset`.
- [x] **[MED]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:873–1160` (`addItemToChecklist` group) — The three error-path tests (`returns null when checklist not found`, `returns null when entity is not a checklist`, `handles exceptions gracefully`) each inline 50–80 lines of setup including a full `ChecklistItem` + `Metadata` construction for `newItem`, even though the path under test terminates before that item's data matters. Extract a `_stubCreateItem(meta)` helper to eliminate ~120 lines of duplication.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the not-found and wrong-type error paths are now one data-driven loop over a `stubCreateItem({checklistId, lookedUpEntity})` helper plus a `buildNonChecklistEntity` factory; ~120 lines of duplicated arrange blocks removed. Bonus: the file turned out to contain wholesale *duplicate groups* (`updateChecklist`, `updateChecklistItem`, `addItemToChecklist` each appeared twice, plus two byte-identical createChecklistItem tests mis-homed in the addItem group) — deduplicated, keeping the two unique tests (atomic create-and-update, addItem-targeted exception path); 12 redundant tests deleted, coverage preserved.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `task_progress_repository_test.dart:211–284` — The batching test uses `fakeAsync` + `.then()` side-effect assignment and reads `resultA?.$1` after `flushMicrotasks()`. This pattern works but is fragile: if the microtask queue is not fully drained the `?.` null-safe access silently passes. Prefer `expect(resultA, isNotNull)` before dereferencing fields, or use `await async.flushFutures()` pattern with an explicit non-null cast.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `task_progress_repository_test.dart:289–520` (`sumTimeSpentFromEntities`) — The 9-test group creates large entity objects inline in each test. A shared `_makeJournalEntry(id, from, to)` factory helper would reduce noise and make the assertions clearer; the pattern repeats verbatim in at least 4 tests.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `TaskProgressRepository.sumTimeSpentFromEntities` (impl: `task_progress_repository.dart:45–57`) is a **pure static function** that maps a `List<JournalEntity>` → `Duration` via union-interval arithmetic. The current suite covers specific fixture cases well, but Glados could verify invariants across arbitrary entity mixes: (a) the result is never negative, (b) adding a fully-contained interval never increases the total, (c) the result is always ≤ the naive sum, and (d) only non-excluded entity types contribute. This is a genuine gap for a non-trivial pure transform.
  **RESOLVED:** done — added a Glados property (numRuns 150) over seed-generated entity mixes (JournalEntry/Task/AiResponseEntry/JournalAudio) asserting all four invariants: non-negative, bounded by the naive sum of counted spans, exclusion-filtering changes nothing, and adding a fully-contained interval never increases the union.
- [x] **[MED]** `TaskProgressRepository._buildTimeRanges` (impl: `task_progress_repository.dart:155–168`) is a private static that maps `List<LinkedEntityTimeSpan>` → `Map<String, TimeRange>`. Although private, it is implicitly tested via `getTaskProgressData`. A Glados property that verifies (a) every input span id appears exactly once as a key, and (b) start/end are preserved, would close the only un-exercised structural invariant.
  **RESOLVED:** done — `_buildTimeRanges` is now `@visibleForTesting buildTimeRanges` (documented later-wins semantics for duplicate ids); a Glados property (numRuns 150) asserts every unique span id appears exactly once with start/end preserved, plus a static duplicate-id later-wins test.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `TaskProgressRepository.getTaskProgress` (impl: `task_progress_repository.dart:91–101`) wraps `calculateUnionDuration`; existing tests already cover overlap-merging cases sufficiently. A generative property is possible but the value is lower here.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `completeChecklistItemsForTask` group (test line 376) — has a `setUp` with stubs but **zero `test(...)` cases**. Whatever the method was supposed to test, that behavior has no coverage at all. Either the tests must be added or the dead group deleted. **RESOLVED:** dead group deleted — the method does not exist in the current impl, so there is no behavior to cover.
- [x] **[MED]** `ChecklistRepository.updateChecklist` with `orElse` branch (impl `checklist_repository.dart:196`) — the `handleNonChecklistEntity` test covers this path (test line 626) but `updateChecklist` always returns `true` even when the entity is wrong type (line 210 of impl). The spec says `false` when not found (line 182), but `true` when the wrong type — this inconsistency in the return value is not explicitly asserted: the test only verifies the error was logged, not the return value semantic. Add an explicit `expect(result, isTrue)` with a code comment explaining the deliberate asymmetry, or reconsider the API.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the wrong-type test already asserted `expect(result, isTrue)`; the missing piece was the rationale, now documented inline: false only means *missing*, a wrong-typed entity logs and returns true via the unconditional `return true` (deliberate asymmetry, non-retriable).
- [x] **[MED]** `ChecklistRepository.getChecklistItemsForTask` — the item-level corrupt-row path (items second bulk read, not parent checklist) is not tested. The impl catches `fromDbEntity` exceptions at line 358; only the parent-checklist corrupt path is exercised (test line 1315). A corrupt item row would be silently skipped and logged; that behavior should be verified.
  **RESOLVED:** done — added 'logs and skips a corrupt ITEM row from the second bulk read while still returning the intact items': checklist resolves, the items read returns one good row + one row with malformed JSON → only the good item returned, error logged once under `getChecklistItemsForTask`.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `TaskProgressRepository._flushPendingTaskProgressBatch` error path (impl line 139–143) — when `getTaskEstimatesByIds` throws, all pending completers are completed with error. The test suite does not cover this path; adding a test that verifies errors propagate correctly to each waiter would close it.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `TaskProgressRepository.getTaskProgressData` dedup / in-flight caching (impl lines 62–87) — the case where the same `id` is requested while a prior request is still in-flight (returning the cached `Future`) is not explicitly tested. This is non-trivial batching logic.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `checklist_repository_test.dart:376–422` — Dead `setUp` in the empty `completeChecklistItemsForTask` group runs mock stub setup on every test run (~6 `when(...)` calls) for zero test coverage. Removing the group eliminates dead CPU cost in every CI run. **RESOLVED:** same as above — the dead setUp is gone.
- [x] **[MED]** `checklist_repository_test.dart` — 1 372 lines in a single file forces the shard runner to load the entire file even when only one group is targeted. After splitting at group boundaries, each shard can run a subset independently.
  **RESOLVED:** done (adapted) — instead of splitting at group boundaries (which would violate the one-test-file-per-source rule), the size problem's actual cause was removed: the file contained full duplicate copies of three groups; after deduplication the file is ~1 100 lines of unique tests for a 400+-line repository, all green.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `task_progress_repository_test.dart:289–520` — Nine tests in `sumTimeSpentFromEntities` each construct fresh `JournalEntry`/`JournalAudio`/`JournalImage` objects inline. A shared top-level `final` fixture would be created once instead of per-test.

---

## Summary

- **1 test file over 1 000 lines** (`checklist_repository_test.dart` at 1 372) — must split into ~6 files.
- **0 impl files over 1 000 lines**.
- **3 weak/dead tests**: empty `completeChecklistItemsForTask` group (no assertions, just dead setup); the dark-mode test only finds a text widget; plus the `updateChecklist` wrong-type return value is not asserted.
- **2 genuine Glados candidates**: `sumTimeSpentFromEntities` (union invariants) and `_buildTimeRanges` (id-key preservation).
- **3 speed wins**: remove dead group setUp; split the 1 372-line file; shared fixture objects for `sumTimeSpentFromEntities` tests.
- **Biggest opportunity**: delete the dead `completeChecklistItemsForTask` group and split `checklist_repository_test.dart` into per-method files — the combination eliminates ~200 lines of dead code and brings the largest test file within guidelines.
